chunks.text: Physical Activity Goal Setting This interactive workshop is designed to empower attendees to take control of their own physical wellbeing. We’ll break down physical activity and explore each of the five components of physical fitness. We’ll introduce a simple test for each component so attendees can take a baseline measure of their fitness at any stage, empowering them to measure and track their physical wellbeing journey. Small impr.

Positive Mental Health Conversations This interactive workshop is designed to support more open conversations in the workplace. The goal is to make mental health conversations that little bit easier by discussing what mental health is and the importance of authenticity, language and listening to your colleagues.   Attendees will also be introduced to an internationally recognised and practical framework for supporting positive mental wellbeing. Workshop Description + Price 3.

The 5 Ways to Wellbeing Introduction to internationally recognised and practical framework for supporting positive mental wellbeing Simple actions to practice each day to maintain or improve our mental health and wellbeing Research from across the world on proven actions that can help us to feel good and function well Each action can make a positive difference to our lives and they are free and easy to incorporate into daily life Workshop Description + Price 2.

## Workshops

Nothing. Membership of the community is free. It’s also free to attend any of our nationwide events or our live webinars (although places can be limited). What’s expected of me if I join the community. You can be as engaged or as passive in the community as you wish. At a minimum we would love if you could come to our events and join our webinars. Our goal is to grow the community nationwide and to represent every workplace in Ireland so your help in spreading the word is greatly appreciated.

The community is for those working directly in workplace health promotion in Ireland, senior leaders and HR professionals interested in promoting wellness in their organisations and anyone that is passionate about making their workplace a healthier place to be. What are the values of the workplace wellbeing ireland community. 1) Share without expecting anything in return 2) Have fun and respect everyone 3) Make friends not contacts How much does it cost t0 join workplace wellbeing ireland.

Workplace Wellbeing Ireland is a one stop shop for all your workplace wellbeing resource, training and education requirements. We don’t believe you should outsource ownership of wellbeing at your workplace to a third party. Our goal is to empower you to manage and measure your own wellbeing programme. It’s a community where you can connect, share ideas and best practice, learn new skills, reach out for support and discuss future trends and innovations. Who is workplace wellbeing ireland for.

By working together, we can build on the momentum and share this message with many more people while at the same time improving the quality of the services that we offer.

The community is for those working directly in workplace health promotion in Ireland, senior leaders and HR professionals interested in promoting wellness in their organisations and anyone that is passionate about making their workplace a healthier place to be. The importance of investing in the health of our workforce is slowly being understood.

Workplace Wellbeing Ireland Workplace Wellbeing Ireland is the location for all your workplace wellbeing resource, education and training requirements. It’s a community that facilitates connection, idea sharing and best practice promotion. It’s a place to learn new skills and to reach out for support and discuss future trends and innovations.

He is the founder of Workplace Wellbeing Ireland and The Work Well Institute and is responsible for the design and delivery of the Postgraduate Certificate in Workplace Wellness at Tangent, Trinity College Dublin. He is the host of The Work Well Podcast. In his spare time Brian is bringing free resistance training to every county and community in Ireland through his parkHIIT project.

## About
Cart 0 About Services Workshops Listen Learn Read Watch Contact Cart 0 About Services Workshops Listen Learn Read Watch Contact by Brian Crooke about       BRIAN CROOKE Brian is a wellbeing educator, speaker and adviser supporting organisations to promote and sustain wellbeing within their workplaces.
As we enter yet another year of uncertainty with continued changes in our work practices, workplace wellbeing is more important now than ever.

Through her advocacy work, public speaking and print, radio and television contributions, Malie shines a light on mental health issues and promotes meaningful wellbeing. Malie lives in Galway with her husband and two little ladies. Malie is a best-selling author with her book ‘Love in, Love out: A compassionate approach to parenting your anxious child’ and she recently created an online course called ' Compassionate Self-Care for Working Parents ' for the Work Well Institute.

Malie Coyne Clinical Psychologist, Author, NUIG Adjunct Psychology Lecturer, ‘A Lust for Life’ Mental Health Advisory Panel, Course Creator at The Work Well Institute With her considerable experience of working with children, families, and clients throughout the lifespan, Malie is fast becoming one of the leading voices in compassionate self-care in Ireland.

Workplace Wellbeing Ireland will continue to promote the importance of wellbeing in Irish workplaces but we can’t do it on our own. We’ve put together a list of people that we believe will have a significant role to play in shaping the future of workplace wellbeing in Ireland and further afield over the coming years. We’ve also gathered some of their thoughts and expectations on what we can expect in 2022.   THE 2022 LIST   Dr.

This was published in December and there is more to come on this in 2022. Government policy: 'Code of Practice on Right to Disconnect' and 'The Right to Request Remote Work Bill'. We can expect a lot more discussion and focus on both of these areas this year. 60 workplace wellbeing leaders of the future graduating from our flagship  8 Steps to Embed a Culture of Health  programme.

At Workplace Wellbeing Ireland, we continue to empower organisations to develop, manage and measure long term meaningful wellbeing programmes and the numbers of students graduating from our programmes at The Work Well Institute in 2021 gives us confidence that our future is in safe hands.   Some major milestones in the past year:  The publication of the first global standard for psychological health and wellbeing in the workplace: ISO45003. The launch of the Healthy Workplace Framework.

Many organisations are placing an increased focus on the wellbeing and mental health of their people. If you don’t have healthy people, you won’t have a healthy business. I still see many “random acts of wellness” masquerading as wellness programmes in Irish workplaces but progress is being made.
